lede: A look at August 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Stiliana Nikolova and Jimmy Uso, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2012–2013 Tana River District clashes
events.desc: Ethnic clashes over grazing rights for cattle in Kenya's Tana River District result in more than 52 deaths.

events.title: Texas Rangers (baseball)
events.desc: The Texas Rangers defeat the Baltimore Orioles 30–3, the most runs scored by a team in modern Major League Baseball history.

events.title: Grigori Perelman
events.desc: Grigori Perelman is awarded the Fields Medal for his proof of the Poincaré conjecture in mathematics but refuses to accept the medal.

events.title: 1985 Manchester Airport disaster
events.desc: British Airtours Flight 28M suffers an engine fire during takeoff at Manchester Airport. The pilots abort but due to inefficient evacuation procedures 55 people are killed, mostly from smoke inhalation.

events.title: District of Columbia Voting Rights Amendment
events.desc: The District of Columbia Voting Rights Amendment is passed by the U.S. Congress, although it is never ratified by a sufficient number of states.

events.title: 1949 Queen Charlotte Islands earthquake
events.desc: The Queen Charlotte earthquake is Canada's strongest since the 1700 Cascadia earthquake.

events.title: First Geneva Convention
events.desc: Twelve nations sign the First Geneva Convention, establishing the rules of protection of the victims of armed conflicts.

events.title: Charles I of England
events.desc: Charles I raises his standard in Nottingham, which marks the beginning of the English Civil War.

events.title: Battle of Bosworth Field
events.desc: The Battle of Bosworth Field occurs; King Richard III of England's death in battle marks the end of the reigning Plantagenet dynasty and the beginning of the Tudors under Henry VII.

births.name: David Reimer
births.line: Canadian man, born male but reassigned female and raised as a girl after a botched circumcision (died 2004)

births.name: Deng Xiaoping
births.line: Chinese soldier and politician, paramount leader and 1st Vice Premier of the People's Republic of China (died 1997)

deaths.text: Gregory G. Pincus, American biologist and academic, co-created the birth-control pill
Robert Gascoyne-Cecil, 3rd Marquess of Salisbury, English academic and politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

observances.text: Christian feast day: Immaculate Heart of Mary (Roman Catholic calendar of 1960)
International Day Commemorating the Victims of Acts of Violence Based on Religion or Belief
Earliest day on which National Heroes' Day (Philippines) can fall, while August 28 is the latest; celebrated on the fourth Monday in August.
